metrics.narrative: Apotex's tail spend is roughly $100M/year, a figure Namish Shah volunteered on the 7 Nov 2025 intro call even without a signed CDA, and the stated driver is gaining control over currently unmanaged tail spend. But no formal business case has been built: Namish Shah pushed back on Zycus's 2-3% recurring-savings guidance as low against 15-20% figures he says he has heard from other tail-spend providers (7 Nov 2025), and no updated or tailored ROI model has been shared since. Metrics exist directionally but the value case itself is contested.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
champion.narrative: Namish Shah has been the consistent point of contact across the 7 Nov 2025 intro call and the 15 Apr 2026 in-person meeting at NAPES, and continues to receive check-ins from Zycus (per the 4 May 2026 Next Step update and the Horizon Sept 2026 event invitation extended to the Apotex team). However, he is Manager-level, has not brokered any introduction to Ryan Mitchell or Carla Baptista, and has not reversed the leadership-level pause on evaluation; his champion strength is developing, not proven, and is at risk given the pause sits above his authority.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is Apotex's incumbent S2P platform ('It's more SAP Ariba for the company' - Namish Shah, 7 Nov 2025), and Zycus positioned directly against it on AI depth during the intro call. Namish Shah also said the team was actively meeting unnamed tail-spend solution providers to understand the market before evaluating fit; none have been named since. The most consequential current alternative is internal: Apotex leadership's decision to consolidate tail spend with existing strategic partners rather than adopt any new vendor, ordered 15 Apr 2026 and still in effect as of this sweep.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
identify_pain.narrative: Namish Shah confirmed on the 7 Nov 2025 intro call that Apotex has significant unmanaged tail spend ('we do have unmanaged spend and we are trying to get some control over that') and that this is specifically why Zycus's tail-spend outreach resonated. The pain is real and sized (an estimated $100M/year in tail spend with limited visibility), though voiced only by the champion, not yet validated by the economic buyer.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started. Namish Shah declined to share the exact tail-spend figure on the 7 Nov 2025 call because no Confidentiality Disclosure Agreement was in place, and Zycus offered to put one in place when Apotex was ready; there is no record that a CDA/NDA has since been signed.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identifiable by name and title: Ryan Mitchell, Head of Procurement, and Carla Baptista, Director of Global Procurement Excellence, are both named in the Next Step field as the leaders who ordered a pause on vendor evaluation on 15 Apr 2026 to consolidate tail spend with strategic partners first. Neither has had any direct engagement with Zycus on any call or email in the record; all contact runs through Namish Shah, a Manager-level champion. The EB is named but not engaged.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
decision_process.narrative: As of the 7 Nov 2025 intro call, Apotex had no formal project or budget, and Namish Shah described the process as informally 'meeting a few solutions providers' before any formal evaluation begins. That informal process was formally paused on 15 Apr 2026 when leadership redirected the team to consolidate spend with strategic partners first, with a stated intent to resume in June 2026; that window passed roughly six weeks ago as of this sweep with no confirmed restart. There is no defined path from pause to decision.

sources: Avoma: Apotex Inc <> Zycus Intro Call, 2025-11-07
decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria have been documented anywhere in the record. The only criterion voiced so far is cost: Namish Shah's skepticism about the 2-3% savings guidance suggests the deal will be judged heavily on quantified savings versus other tail-spend providers, but no written requirements, weighting, or rubric has surfaced.
